risk_signals: External integrations are present and will need interface contracts plus vendor coordination.
Compliance-oriented requirements are present and should be validated early to avoid late rework.
Non-functional scope is substantial, so quality engineering should run in parallel with feature delivery.
3 high-complexity tasks sit on the roadmap and deserve early prototyping.

committee_brief.domain_inference: Likely domain: commerce and transactional order management.
committee_brief.scope_assessment: Scope includes 5 functional task(s) and 3 non-functional task(s); 0 task(s) sit outside the confirmed baseline because they are optional or low-confidence.
assumption_log: Dependency sequencing was inferred for 4 task(s) to produce an executable implementation order beyond the raw prose order.
committee_brief.confidence_signal: Planning confidence is high: no low-confidence tasks were detected in the current plan.
committee_brief.graph_summary: 8 requirements -> 8 atomic tasks across 4 delivery sprints. Critical delivery path: T006 -> T003 -> T004 -> T005 (4 hops, ~12 story points, estimated 5 weeks with a 2-person team). Coordination hotspot: Optimize scalability for 10,000 concurrent users blocks 4 downstream tasks and should be closed by Sprint 1. Parallel execution opportunity: 4 tasks can start immediately in Sprint 1. Effort breakdown: 236 estimated hours | FR: 120h | NFR: 116h.
